title: dactylorhiza × aschersoniana nothosubsp. templinensis
intro: Dactylorhiza × aschersoniana nothosubsp. templinensis is a hybrid orchid species, resulting from the cross between Dactylorhiza incarnata and Coeloglossum viride, characterized by its unique appearance and growth habits. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are derived from its flavonoid and phenolic compounds. The health benefits of Dactylorhiza × aschersoniana nothosubsp. templinensis include its potential in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or tincture, and its extracts are used in herbal preparations to support respiratory health and boost the immune system.
